title: Panya - Wisdom and Intuitive Intelligence
justification: This talk directly addresses the chapter theme with precise focus on paññā (wisdom/intuitive intelligence), explaining its etymology, nature, and relationship to awareness. It offers a coherent philosophical framework distinguishing wisdom from mere knowledge, explores how this intelligence expresses through physical, aesthetic, and intellectual domains, and connects it beautifully to vipassana practice. The talk has excellent literary potential with memorable examples (the tennis player, the artist, the tongue tasting curry) and maintains a clear, accessible voice throughout.
summary: Bhante explores the Pali term paññā as intuitive intelligence that underlies all knowing, distinguishing it from conceptual knowledge. He explains how vipassana practice helps disembed this primordial awareness from its confusion with body, heart, and mind, revealing our fundamental Buddha nature.

relevance.note: Directly explores paññā as intuitive intelligence and wisdom
